---
title: "Clustering_DT_Calling_NSCLCORIEN"
author: "Dipankor Dhrubo"
date: "2025-10-21"
output: html_document
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(knitr)
library(tidyverse)
library(umap)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(factoextra)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)
library(forcats)
```


```{r}

## Creating the binary data for clustering

##  Loading of data

NSCLC_metadata<- read_rds("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Data/NSCLC_metadata.rds")

timeline_long_norm<- read_csv("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Data/NSCLCORIEN_Treatment_simplified_longformat_data.csv")


# Step 1: Define the time bin
treatment_matrix <- timeline_long_norm %>%
  select(sample, TimeSinceTreatmentStart, treatment_group) %>%
  mutate(
    TimeBin = sprintf("Month_%03d", round(TimeSinceTreatmentStart * 12)),
    treatment_group = as.character(treatment_group)
  ) %>%
  group_by(sample, TimeBin) %>%
  summarise(
    Treatment = paste(sort(unique(treatment_group)), collapse = "+"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = TimeBin,
    values_from = Treatment,
    values_fill = NA
  )


# Get column names without SL_ID
month_cols <- names(treatment_matrix)[names(treatment_matrix) != "sample"]

# Extract just the numeric part from the month columns
month_numbers <- as.numeric(stringr::str_extract(month_cols, "\\d+"))

# Order column names based on numeric month values
ordered_months <- month_cols[order(month_numbers)]

# Reorder your matrix columns
treatment_matrix_ordered <- treatment_matrix[, c("sample", ordered_months)]



treatment_long <- treatment_matrix_ordered %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -sample, names_to = "Month", values_to = "Treatment") %>%
  mutate(Treatment = replace_na(Treatment, "None"))

# One-hot encode treatments
treatment_encoded <- treatment_long %>%
  mutate(Feature = paste(Month, Treatment, sep = "_"),
         Value = 1) %>%
  select(sample, Feature, Value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Feature, values_from = Value, values_fill = list(Value = 0))

write_csv(treatment_encoded,"/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Data/NSCLCORIEN_Treatment_binary_data.csv")

```


```{r}

## Clustering for 5 years timerline

treatment_short <- treatment_matrix_ordered %>% select(1:62) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -sample, names_to = "Month", values_to = "Treatment") %>%
  mutate(Treatment = replace_na(Treatment, "None"))

# One-hot encode treatments
treatment_encoded_short <- treatment_short %>%
  mutate(Feature = paste(Month, Treatment, sep = "_"),
         Value = 1) %>%
  select(sample, Feature, Value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Feature, values_from = Value, values_fill = list(Value = 0))


# Step 2: Separate SL_ID and treatment features
sl_ids <- treatment_encoded_short$sample
features <- treatment_encoded_short %>% select(-sample)

# Step 3: PCA (reduce dimensionality before distance calculation)
pca_result_short <- prcomp(features, scale. = TRUE)
pca_data_short <- as.data.frame(pca_result_short$x[, 1:50])
pca_matrix <- as.matrix(pca_data_short)# First 50 PCs
rownames(pca_matrix) <- sl_ids


# Step 4: Run UMAP
set.seed(42)
umap_result <- umap(pca_matrix, n_neighbors = 30, min_dist = 0.3, metric = "euclidean")
umap_df <- as.data.frame(umap_result$layout)
colnames(umap_df) <- c("UMAP1", "UMAP2")


### Clustering
cluster_results <- data.frame(sample = rownames(umap_df))

# Loop over different k values
for (k in 3:20) {
  set.seed(42)
  km <- kmeans(umap_df, centers = k)
  cluster_results[[paste0("Cluster_k", k)]] <- km$cluster
}


colnames(cluster_results)[1]<- "sample"


## Adding survival data to clustering file 

Surv_data<- NSCLC_metadata %>% filter(sample %in% cluster_results$sample) %>% select(sample, diagsurvtime,status,Candida,Normalize_Candida,CAlevel)

Cluster_surv<- cluster_results %>% 
  left_join(Surv_data,by="sample") %>% drop_na() %>% unique() 

write_csv(Cluster_surv,"/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Data/Cluster_survival_NSCLC.csv")

```

```{r}

## MTsurv plotting for all the clusters

cluster_cols <- grep("^Cluster_k", names(Cluster_surv), value = TRUE)

plot_list <- list()

for (cluster in cluster_cols) {
  # Make sure it's a factor
  Cluster_surv[[cluster]] <- as.factor(Cluster_surv[[cluster]])
  
  # Survival object
  surv_obj <- Surv(time = Cluster_surv$diagsurvtime, event = Cluster_surv$status)
  
  # Fit model
  fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ Cluster_surv[[cluster]])
  
  # Create plot
  plot <- ggsurvplot(
    fit,
    data = Cluster_surv,
    pval = TRUE,
    title = paste("Survival Plot for", cluster),
    legend.title = "Cluster",
    legend.labs = levels(Cluster_surv[[cluster]]),
    palette = "Dark2"
  )$plot
  
  plot_list[[length(plot_list) + 1]] <- plot
}

# Combine all plots using patchwork
combined_plot <- wrap_plots(plotlist = plot_list, ncol = 5)

# Display all plots
combined_plot

ggsave("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Figures/Cluster_Survival.png", plot = combined_plot, width = 30, height = 26, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}



# Expect these columns in Cluster_normal_Surv:
# sample, diagsurvtime, status (1=event, 0=censor), Total_count2, Cluster_k3...Cluster_k20

df0 <- Cluster_surv %>%
  transmute(
    sample,
    days        = as.numeric(diagsurvtime),
    vitalstatus = as.integer(status),
    Normalize_Candida,
    across(starts_with("Cluster_k"), .names = "{.col}")
  )

stopifnot(all(c("sample","days","vitalstatus","Normalize_Candida") %in% names(df0)))

# mt.surv input frames (global)
surv_df  <- df0 %>% select(sample, vitalstatus, days)
value_df <- df0 %>% select(sample, Normalize_Candida)

# Output dir
out_dir <- "/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Figures/mtsurv_scans_by_k"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Collect all scans here
mts_all_df <- tibble()

# Loop over k columns and clusters
k_cols <- grep("^Cluster_k\\d+$", names(df0), value = TRUE)

for (k_col in k_cols) {
  message("Scanning ", k_col)
  
  # add cluster label for this k
  df_k <- df0 %>%
    select(sample, Normalize_Candida, !!k_col) %>%
    rename(cluster = !!k_col) %>%
    filter(!is.na(cluster))
  
  for (cl in sort(unique(df_k$cluster))) {
    ids <- df_k %>% filter(cluster == cl) %>% pull(sample)
    
    v_sub <- value_df %>% semi_join(tibble(sample = ids), by = "sample")
    s_sub <- surv_df  %>% semi_join(tibble(sample = ids), by = "sample")
    
    # mtsurv scan (positional args)
    mts <- mt.surv::survivalByQuantile("Normalize_Candida", v_sub, s_sub) %>%
      mutate(
        log10p   = -log10(pval),
        k_col    = k_col,
        cluster  = as.character(cl)
      )
    
    # mark chosen percentile (max -log10p with HR>=1; fallback = min p)
    best <- mts %>% filter(hazard.direction == ">=1", is.finite(log10p))
    if (nrow(best) == 0) best <- mts %>% arrange(pval) %>% slice_head(n = 1)
    chosen_pct <- best$percentile[1]
    
    mts <- mts %>% mutate(chosen = percentile == chosen_pct)
    
    mts_all_df <- bind_rows(mts_all_df, mts)
  }
}

# Save raw scans (optional)
readr::write_csv(mts_all_df, file.path(out_dir, "mtsurv_scans_all_clusters.csv"))


plot_mtsurv_per_k <- function(mts_all_df, out_dir = "mtsurv_scans_by_k") {
  mts_all_df %>%
    split(.$k_col) %>%
    purrr::iwalk(function(dk,k) {
      
      # pick the max -log10(p) **within the HR≥1 (green) curve** for each cluster
      chosen_pts <- dk %>%
        dplyr::filter(hazard.direction == ">=1", is.finite(log10p)) %>%
        dplyr::group_by(cluster) %>%
        dplyr::slice_max(order_by = log10p, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
        dplyr::ungroup()
      
      # fallback: if a cluster has no HR≥1 points, take the overall smallest p
      missing_cl <- setdiff(unique(dk$cluster), unique(chosen_pts$cluster))
      if (length(missing_cl)) {
        dk_fallback <- dk %>%
          dplyr::filter(cluster %in% missing_cl, is.finite(log10p)) %>%
          dplyr::group_by(cluster) %>%
          dplyr::slice_max(order_by = log10p, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
          dplyr::ungroup()
        chosen_pts <- dplyr::bind_rows(chosen_pts, dk_fallback)
      }
      
      # order facets numerically if cluster labels are numeric
      dk <- dk %>% dplyr::mutate(cluster = factor(cluster, levels = sort(unique(as.numeric(cluster)))))
      chosen_pts <- chosen_pts %>% dplyr::mutate(cluster = factor(cluster, levels = levels(dk$cluster)))
      
      p <- ggplot(dk, aes(percentile, log10p, color = hazard.direction, group = cluster)) +
        geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
        geom_point(data = chosen_pts, aes(percentile, log10p),
                   size = 2.2, color = "black", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed") +
        facet_wrap(~ cluster, scales = "free_y") +
        theme_bw() +
        labs(title = paste("mtsurv scans —", k),
             x = "Percentile", y = expression(-log[10](p)))
      
      ggsave(file.path(out_dir, paste0("mtsurv_scans_", k, ".png")),
             p, width = 14, height = 8, dpi = 300)
    })
}
plot_mtsurv_per_k(mts_all_df, out_dir = out_dir)


```
